research_question: Can a portfolio-extraction-only challenger freeze the v18 two-signal score family, calibrated liquidity guard, and mild refresh hysteresis, but replace equal-weight refreshed holdings with a deterministic piecewise bucket extraction that reduces incumbent resize churn enough to improve cost robustness without giving back the v17/v18 return and drawdown gains?

score_contract_frozen.score_rule: mean(percentile_rank(momentum_60_5_raw DESC), percentile_rank(liquidity_trend_20_60_raw DESC)); require min_feature_count >= 2

guard_contract.guard_definition: Freeze the calibrated v7 / v17 ranking guard: only rows that are already ranking_eligible_D0 and whose 20-day average amount cross-sectional liquidity rank is >= 0.70 on the same signal_date may enter the ranking universe.
guard_contract.treatment_of_rows_below_guard: Remain in audit tables but must be excluded from ranking_state_daily eligibility.

portfolio_refresh_contract_frozen.refresh_definition: Freeze the v18 incumbent-retention hysteresis: retain an incumbent if it still has rank_position <= 15 on the new signal_date and still satisfies the frozen guard plus tradability requirements. Only vacated slots may be refilled from the highest-ranked non-held eligible names until 10 names are held.
portfolio_refresh_contract_frozen.replacement_rule: Fill remaining slots with highest-ranked non-held eligible names in ascending rank order until 10 names are held.
portfolio_refresh_contract_frozen.target_weight_rule: Equal-weight across currently held names after refresh; unfilled slots remain cash.

portfolio_extraction_contract.extraction_definition: After the frozen v18 refresh rule selects the held names for each signal_date, sort the selected names by current rank_position and assign deterministic per-name bucket weights within the refreshed portfolio: ranks 1-3 get 0.15 each, ranks 4-6 get 0.10 each, and ranks 7-10 get 0.0625 each. Normalize active selected weights to sum to 1.0 when fewer than 10 names are held.
portfolio_extraction_contract.treatment_of_ranks_above_head_size: Ranks above 10 may remain selected by the frozen refresh rule but receive zero extraction weight until renormalization over active selected names.

constants_frozen.selection_contract: same v18 score family, same calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard, and same top-15 refresh hysteresis

motivation_from_v17_to_v19_evidence: v17 showed portfolio_extraction can materially improve annual_relative_return, relative_ir, max_drawdown, and low-liquidity weight share versus v16, but turnover rose sharply.
v18 showed mild refresh hysteresis preserved those extraction gains but did not reduce turnover.
v19 showed a direct stability cap also failed to reduce turnover, implying the main issue may be continuous cross-rank resizing inside the refreshed head rather than replacement flow alone.
disallowed_changes: Do not change score features or score weights.
Do not change the calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard threshold or its stage.
Do not change the frozen top-15 refresh hysteresis rule.
Do not change benchmark or cost contracts.
Do not change sample labels or return definitions.
Do not sweep multiple bucket layouts.
Do not sweep multiple extraction formulas.
Do not combine this round with score-family, refresh-rule, or weight-penalty changes.
notes: This round changes exactly one core dimension: portfolio extraction.
The purpose is to preserve the productive v17/v18 direction while replacing continuous resized weights with a coarse bucket schedule that should be less sensitive to small day-to-day rank reshuffles.
